mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower real rates compress required returns, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.37% as of 2016-05-01 indicates near-zero rates post-crisis
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during easing periods do not exceed baseline by at least 1.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market tightening that increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 143,894k as of 2016-05-01 indicates sustained employment growth
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer demand and construction activity, indicating strong aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 455,644M and HOUST at 1,172k as of 2016-04-01
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized demand expansion
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over 2 months signals supply-side weakness or demand destruction that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.55 (vintage) vs 98.42 (revised) as of 2016-05-01 indicates significant downward revision risk
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or mean absolute return magnitude is less than 0.8% in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5% year-over-year) and elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 1.5%) signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 238.89 and FEDFUNDS at 0.37% as of 2016-05-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. This mechanism is strongest in mid-cycle expansions before inflation concerns emerge.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,505.1B (vintage) vs 19,001.69B (revised) as of 2016-01-01 indicates significant revision risk
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or mean forward return is not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) over a 2-month window signals synchronized demand weakness that portends earnings deterioration and equity market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,172k and RSAFS at 455,644M as of 2016-04-01
regime_hint: demand shock or recession onset with synchronized contraction
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
